LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF LEONARD VILLAFLOR, AND, JANET VILLAFLOR MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICTION. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED. PLAINTIFFS FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. WHILE THE COURT BELIEVES THAT DEFENDANT'S REFORMATION CLAIM IS TENUOUS, THE COURT CANNOT GRANT SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE CITED CLAIMS. THE COURT'S RULING WILL NOT COMPLETELY DISPOSE OF THE CLAIMS. SEE CCP SECTION 437C(F)(1). PLAINTIFFS' SEPARATE STATEMENT DOES NOT ADEQUATELY SET FORTH WHAT AMOUNTS ARE OWED AND THE TRIAL JUDGE WILL NEED TO MAKE COMPENSATORY ADJUSTMENTS REGARDING THE RENTS, PROPERTY TAXES, AND TIME AND EFFORT THAT DEFENDANT HAS EXPENDED IN MANAGING THE PREMISES. (DEFENSE COUNSEL WILL PREPARE PROPOSED ORDER WHICH TRACKS THE TENTATIVE RULING AND ATTACH SEPARATE STATEMENTS OF UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS TO ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE CSR 5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF LEONARD VILLAFLOR, AND, JANET VILLAFLOR MOTION FOR TERMINATING AND MONETARY SANCTIONS AND ORDER FOR SALE AND DISTRIBUTION. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTION AND ORDER FOR SALE AND DISTRIBUTION IS DENIED. MOTION FOR MONETARY SANCTION IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT VIOLATED LOCAL RULE 4.3 M BY HIS WILLFUL FAILURE TO APPEAR AT THE EARLY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE WITHOUT JUST CAUSE. DEFENDANT MUST PAY SANCTIONS TO PLAINTIFFS IN THE AMOUNT OF $9,290.00 WHICH REPRESENTS PLAINTIFFS' COUNSELS' REASONABLE FEES AND COSTS SPENT IN PREPARATION FOR AND ATTENDANCE AT THE CONFERENCE, AND IN BRINGING THIS MOTION. (CCP SECTION 575.2; LYLE DECLARATION.)(PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE CSR 5797

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Disquality Attorney Rebert H. Peterson Iii
Off Calendar Jul-13-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS LEONARD VILLAFLOR AND JANET VILLAFLOR'S Motion to Disqualify Attorney Robert H. Peterson III. Appearances: Matthew D. Zumstein, Esq. of Ropers Majeski, (650) 780-1641, appearing for Plaintiff and moving party; Robert H. Peterson III, Law Offices of Robert H. Peterson III, appearing for Defendant and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: granted. Attorney Robert H. Peterson, III is disqualified from further representation of defendant and cross-complainant pursuant of California Rules of Professional Conduct 5-210 and 3-700 due to counsel's status as a necessary material witness in the matter. The Court adds: this ruling will become effective 7-17-09, unless the matter settles at the settlement conference on 7-16-09. (The stay of ruling until 7-17-09 allows Mr. Peterson to attend the Settlement conference on 7-16-09 and represent his client.) Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-01-2009 Continued To Jul-16-09 At 03:30 P.M. In Dept 501

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-25-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF LEONARD VILLAFLOR, and, JANET VILLAFLOR MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICTION. Appearances: Robert H. Peterson, III, Esq. of Peterson & Peterson, (415) 586-7460, appearing for Defendant and opposing party; There are no other appearances. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for Summary Judgment denied. Plaintiffs failed to shift the burden of proof. While the Court believes that defendant's reformation claim is tenuous, the Court cannot grant summary adjudication on the cited claims. The Court's ruling will not completely dispose of the claims. See CCP section 437c(f)(1). Plaintiffs' separate statement does not adequately set forth what amounts are owed and the trial judge will need to make compensatory adjustments regarding the rents, property taxes, and time and effort that defendant has expended in managing the premises. (Defense counsel will prepare proposed Order which tracks the tentative ruling and attach separate statements of undisputed material facts to Order.)(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Ntc And Mtn For Terminating And Monetary Sanctions And Order For Sale And Distribution
Off Calendar Jun-25-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF LEONARD VILLAFLOR, and, JANET VILLAFLOR Motion for Terminating and Monetary Sanctions and Order for Sale and Distribution. Appearances: Robert H. Peterson, III, Esq. of Peterson & Peterson, (415) 586-7460, appearing for Defendant and opposing party; There are no other appearances. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for terminating sanction and order for sale and distribution is denied. Motion for monetary sanction is granted. Defendant violated Local Rule 4.3 M by his willful failure to appear at the Early Settlement Conference without just cause. Defendant must pay sanctions to plaintiffs in the amount of $9,290.00 which represents plaintiffs' counsels' reasonable fees and costs spent in preparation for and attendance at the conference, and in bringing this motion. (CCP section 575.2; Lyle declaration.)(Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
